### Recordings, Inc. v. Perlman (1984)

Recordings, Inc. v. Perlman (1984) is a landmark case in copyright law that deals with the question of whether a specific performance of a musical composition is protected by copyright. The court ruled that a performance of a musical composition is not protected by copyright, and the specific performance can be freely copied and distributed. This ruling has significant implications for the music industry and the way music is consumed and distributed.
